name: Ventoso y embalse de El Juncal – circular desde Nocina por la ría de Oriñón
region_intro: Mountain biking around Guriezo offers diverse terrain within Cantabria, Spain, characterized by its mountainous landscape and the Agüera river valley. The region features a mix of challenging ascents, green forests, and open meadows, providing varied routes for cyclists. Its proximity to the Cantabric Sea also allows for routes with coastal views and access to nearby beaches. This geographical setting supports a network of trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Guriezo The most popular mountain bike trail is Pico de las Nieves por el Parque Natural de Armañón – circular desde Nocina por embalse de El Juncal, a 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete. This route includes an ascent to Pico de las Nieves, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ventoso y embalse de El Juncal – circular desde Nocina por la ría de Oriñón, a difficult 28.6 miles (46.0 km) path. This trail explores the Ventoso area and the El Juncal reservoir, with views towards the Oriñón estuary. Local mountain bikers also love the Alto de LA-1 (395 m) – Antenas de La Hoz loop from Rioseco, a 26.0 miles (41.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and past communication antennas, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Mountain biking around Guriezo is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and green forests, with routes often featuring significant elevation gain and views of the Cantabric Sea. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Guriezo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Guriezo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Guriezo?
A: There are 10 mountain bike trails around Guriezo, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Guriezo?
A: Mountain biking in Guriezo features diverse terrain, from challenging ascents in mountainous areas to rides through green forests and open meadows. The region is characterized by imposing rocky mountains, the Agüera river valley, and offers routes with stunning panoramic views, sometimes extending to the Cantabric Sea.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Guriezo?
A: While Guriezo is known for its more challenging routes, there are 4 moderate mountain bike trails available. These routes provide a good option for those looking for a less strenuous ride compared to the 6 difficult trails in the area.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while mountain biking in Guriezo?
A: Guriezo's trails often pass through areas with significant natural beauty. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1834895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye&lt;/a&gt;, a unique natural rock formation, or enjoy views of the El Juncal Reservoir. The region also features impressive cliffs and panoramic viewpoints like those from Pico de las Nieves or Cerredo Mountain, offering vistas of the Cantabric Sea.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Guriezo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Guriezo are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, with no routes specifically listed as easy. Families with younger or less experienced riders might find the terrain challenging. It's recommended to check the specific route details for elevation gain and technical difficulty to ensure it suits your family's skill level.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Guriezo?
A: Many natural areas in Cantabria, including those around Guriezo, are generally dog-friendly, especially on trails. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas you plan to visit.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Guriezo?
A: Guriezo offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. While summer can be warm, the mountainous terrain often offers cooler air. Winter riding is possible, but riders should be prepared for potentially wet or colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Guriezo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Guriezo are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999664795/pico-de-las-nieves-por-el-parque-natural-de-armanon-circular-desde-nocina-por-embalse-de-el-juncal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de las Nieves por el Parque Natural de Armañón – circular desde Nocina por embalse de El Juncal&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999659773/ventoso-y-embalse-de-el-juncal-circular-desde-nocina-por-la-ria-de-orinon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventoso y embalse de El Juncal – circular desde Nocina por la ría de Oriñón&lt;/a&gt; are popular loop trails.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Guriezo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Guriezo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging ascents, and the stunning panoramic views of both the mountains and the Cantabric Sea.

Q: Are there opportunities for other outdoor activities near the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. Beyond mountain biking, Guriezo and its surroundings offer trekking, mountaineering, and even speleology (cave exploration). The Agüera river estuary is suitable for canoeing, and the area is excellent for nature watching, including birdwatching of vulture colonies and migratory birds. You can also visit nearby beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/577101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Arena Beach&lt;/a&gt; for a coastal experience.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails in Guriezo typically take to complete?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Guriezo varies depending on the route and your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999664795/pico-de-las-nieves-por-el-parque-natural-de-armanon-circular-desde-nocina-por-embalse-de-el-juncal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de las Nieves por el Parque Natural de Armañón&lt;/a&gt; trail typically takes around 3 hours 28 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999659773/ventoso-y-embalse-de-el-juncal-circular-desde-nocina-por-la-ria-de-orinon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventoso y embalse de El Juncal&lt;/a&gt; route can take approximately 4 hours 46 minutes due to its longer distance and higher elevation gain.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the coast or nearby beaches?
A: Yes, Guriezo's proximity to the Cantabric Sea means some routes offer coastal views. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999659773/ventoso-y-embalse-de-el-juncal-circular-desde-nocina-por-la-ria-de-orinon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventoso y embalse de El Juncal – circular desde Nocina por la ría de Oriñón&lt;/a&gt; trail provides views towards the Oriñón estuary. After your ride, you can easily access nearby beaches like Oriñon, Arenillas, or Sonabia.
tips: A little respite enjoying the views of Eastern Cantabria before continuing with the route.
At the side of the path and at the foot of the Alto de Anguia is this menhir surrounded by a beautiful landscape, an ideal place to take a break and enjoy the views.

At 731 meters above sea level, the top of El Ventoso guarantees wonderful panoramic views of the entire interior of Cantabria and the Basque Country. It is a very popular site and certainly worth visiting.
A very quiet and picturesque place surrounded by a beautiful landscape typical of this area of the Cantabrian interior. It is worth turning it around on the track that runs through it.
Pleasant track along the banks of the Agüera river. There is a picnic area with swings and access to the river.

waypoints.name: Trail along the banks of the Agüera River
